title: Banana Nice Cream
description: Two-ingredient frozen banana soft-serve. A cold craving fix with no dairy and no ice-cream maker.
explanation: Frozen ripe bananas blend into a soft-serve texture that scratches the ice-cream itch. Cocoa, peanut butter, or berries turn it into dessert without refined sugar if the bananas are ripe enough.
Freeze banana slices flat on a tray first so they do not clump into one ice brick.
ingredients: 3 ripe Bananas, sliced and frozen solid
1 tbsp Cocoa powder or Peanut Butter (optional)
Toppings: nuts, cacao nibs, or berries
instructions: Add frozen banana slices to a strong blender or food processor.
Blend, scraping sides, adding plant milk a spoon at a time until creamy.
Add cocoa or peanut butter if using; blend again.
Serve immediately as soft-serve, or freeze 30–60 minutes for a firmer scoop.
stepNotes: Bananas must be fully frozen or the texture stays slushy-sweet rather than creamy.
A food processor often works better than a weak blender.
Do not add too much liquid or it becomes a smoothie.
Eat soon—it melts faster than dairy ice cream.
Very ripe spotted bananas taste sweetest with no added sugar.

variationSections.title: Flavor riffs
items: Mango banana with frozen mango chunks.

variationSections.title: Protein dessert
items: Add a scoop of plant protein and extra splash of soy milk.
Swirl peanut butter and top with crushed roasted peanuts.
Serve with a side of soy curd for contrast.
